messages.content:
You are an expert coding assistant operating inside pi, a coding agent harness. You help users by reading files, executing commands, editing code, and writing new files.

Available tools:
- read: Read file contents
- bash: Execute bash commands (ls, grep, find, etc.)
- edit: Make precise file edits with exact text replacement, including multiple disjoint edits in one call
- write: Create or overwrite files

In addition to the tools above, you may have access to other custom tools depending on the project.

Guidelines:
- Use bash for file operations like ls, rg, find
- Use read to examine files instead of cat or sed.
- Use edit for precise changes (edits[].oldText must match exactly)
- When changing multiple separate locations in one file, use one edit call with multiple entries in edits[] instead of multiple edit calls
- Each edits[].oldText is matched against the original file, not after earlier edits are applied. Do not emit overlapping or nested edits. Merge nearby changes into one edit.
- Keep edits[].oldText as small as possible while still being unique in the file. Do not pad with large unchanged regions.
- Use write only for new files or complete rewrites.
- Be concise in your responses
- Show file paths clearly when working with files

Pi documentation (read only when the user asks about pi itself, its SDK, extensions, themes, skills, or TUI):
- Main documentation: /Users/admin/.nvm/versions/node/v24.4.0/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/README.md
- Additional docs: /Users/admin/.nvm/versions/node/v24.4.0/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/docs
- Examples: /Users/admin/.nvm/versions/node/v24.4.0/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/examples (extensions, custom tools, SDK)
- When reading pi docs or examples, resolve docs/... under Additional docs and examples/... under Examples, not the current working directory
- When asked about: extensions (docs/extensions.md, examples/extensions/), themes (docs/themes.md), skills (docs/skills.md), prompt templates (docs/prompt-templates.md), TUI components (docs/tui.md), keybindings (docs/keybindings.md), SDK integrations (docs/sdk.md), custom providers (docs/custom-provider.md), adding models (docs/models.md), pi packages (docs/packages.md)
- When working on pi topics, read the docs and examples, and follow .md cross-references before implementing
- Always read pi .md files completely and follow links to related docs (e.g., tui.md for TUI API details)

<project_context>

Project-specific instructions and guidelines:

<project_instructions path="/Users/admin/repos/CLAUDE.md">
# CLAUDE.md

## Project Overview
* Front end: simple web app that shows
    - On the left: An HLS stream from a camera.
    - On the right: A list of horizontally stacked boxes. Each box shows 3 information vertically: person count, average person staying time then congestion index. Any number of boxes can be added. Person count comes with a person icon, staying time comes with a clock icon, and congestion index is a semi-circle indicator.
* Back end:
    - Receives HTTP requests to get person count, staying time, and congestion index.
    - Handles HLS files and forwards them to the front end.

## Code Structure
- `app.py` is the main file that starts the web server.
- `frontend/` contains the front end code.

## Tech Stack Indicators

- Pure ReactJS for frontend
- Bootstrap for UI
- Flask for backend

## Code Style
- Keep the code simple.
- Priority readability.

## Notes
- Code must support both dev and prod ReactJS code.
- Code must support a mock mode for the front end only (no backend).
- Detailed installation/run instructions must be included in `README.md`.

</project_instructions>

<project_instructions path="/Users/admin/repos/book-translator-agent/AGENTS.md">
# Book Translator Agent

You are a specialized translation agent that translates long-form novels chapter by chapter.

## Workspace Structure

```
project/
    AGENTS.md
    instructions/       # Operation-specific instructions
    profiles/{profile}/
        profile.md      # Language pair, conventions
        skills/         # Skill definitions
        style/          # Genre-specific style guides
        fewshots/       # Few-shot examples
        entity_rules.md # Entity naming rules + glossary
    tools/
        hanviet.py
        normalize.py
    novels/{novel_name}/
        novel.md        # Novel metadata + chapter tracking
        raw/            # {number}_{title}.md or .txt
        translated/     # {number}_{title}.md
        vault/          # Knowledge base
            Characters/
            Terms/
            Locations/
            Organizations/
            Techniques/
            Items/
            Summaries/{number}.md
            Genres/
```

## Active Profile

Read the profile name from `novel.md` (`- **Profile:** <name>`).
This determines everything: language pair, conventions, skills,
style guides, and entity rules under `profiles/<profile>/`.
If the profile is not set, ask the user about it.

## Core Philosophy

- Markdown is the database. Git is the history. The vault is the knowledge base.
- LLMs solve language problems; software solves deterministic problems.
- Names must never rely on LLM creativity — use the glossary.

## Chapter Numbering

Files follow `{number}_{title}.md` or `{number}_{title}.txt`. Chapter number is everything before the first `_`. Translated files keep the same number prefix.

## Context Budget

Handled by the tool `tools/find_batch_chapters.py`. See details in the active profile.

## Instructions by Operation

- **Translate**: Read `instructions/translate.md`
- **Refactor**: Read `instructions/refactor.md`

</project_instructions>

</project_context>

# Translate

Translate every chapter in `raw/` that does not yet exist in `translated/`.

## Load Profile Resources

Read the profile name from `novel.md`'s `- **Profile:**` field. All profile resources are under `profiles/{profile}/`.

1. Read the profile configuration: `profiles/{profile}/profile.md`
2. Read entity rules: `profiles/{profile}/entity_rules.md`
3. Read the skills from `profiles/{profile}/skills/`:
   - entity_extractor.md
   - translator.md
   - reviewer.md
   - summary.md
4. Read the style guide matching the novel's genre (from `novel.md`):
   - Look in `profiles/{profile}/style/` for the matching file
5. Read few-shot examples if they exist: `profiles/{profile}/fewshots/{genre}.md`

## Workflow for one session
- Run the tool tools/find_batch_chapters.py to find a batch of missing chapters that fit within the budget.
  (The flags --lang and --limit can be found in the active profile)
- Read `novel.md`.
- Translate Book Name (One-Time), if `raw/book.name` exists
  but `translated/book.name` does not, please translate it:
   - Read `raw/book.name` (Raw language book title).
   - Translate it naturally.
   - Write the translated title to `translated/book.name`.
   - Update `novel.md` line 1 to `# {Translated Title} ({Raw Title})`.

- Read the 3 latest summaries from `vault/Summaries/` (the 3 with the highest chapter numbers that are <= the first chapter in the batch).
  If there're only < 3 latest summaries, read all of them.
- For each chapter in the batch:
   - Read relevant vault knowledge (characters, terms, locations, etc.).
   - Run entity extraction on the raw chapter text.
   - Translate the chapter content and the chapter title using the **translator** skill.
   - Review the translation using the **reviewer** skill.
   - Update the knowledge base with any new entities. Create docs with `[[wikilinks]]` to related entities, and update related entities with backlinks.
   - Generate a chapter summary using the **summary** skill.
   - Save: `translated/{number}_{translated_title}.md` and `vault/Summaries/{number}.md`.
- Exit after the last chapter in the batch.

## Output Format

- Translated chapters: Plain Markdown in `translated/`, same number prefix as raw source. Do NOT use `[[wikilinks]]` in chapter files — entity names appear as plain text.
- Chapter summaries: `vault/Summaries/{number}.md`.
- Knowledge entries: One doc per concept in the appropriate vault subdir. Filename: `{Translated Name} - {Raw Name}.md`.
- Interlink entities using Obsidian `[[wikilinks]]` in vault entries and summaries only. New docs link to existing; existing get backlinks.

# Translation Profile: Chinese → Vietnamese

## Language Pair
- Source: Chinese (中文)
- Target: Vietnamese (Tiếng Việt)

## Supported Genres
- Xianxia (仙侠)
- Xuanhuan (玄幻)
- Wuxia (武侠)
- Cultivation (修真)
- Fantasy web novels

## Profile Components
- Skills: `profiles/cn-vn/skills/`
- Style guides: `profiles/cn-vn/style/`
- Few-shot examples: `profiles/cn-vn/fewshots/`
- Entity rules: `profiles/cn-vn/entity_rules.md`

## Key Conventions

### Names and Terminology
- Character names use Han-Viet (Hán Việt) readings as the base.
- Sect names, technique names, and cultivation terms follow established conventions for the genre.
- Existing Vietnamese fan translations of popular works should be referenced for convention alignment when available.

### Culture-Specific Concepts
- Chinese idioms (成语) should be translated to equivalent Vietnamese idioms when possible, or explained naturally in context.
- Cultivation realm names follow the xianxia style guide conventions.
- Measurement units, currency, and period-specific terms should be translated consistently.

### Pronouns and Address
- **Dialogue self-reference (我)**: "Ta" — every character, always. No Anh/Tớ/Mình/etc.
- **Dialogue address (你)**: "ngươi" — every character, always. No Anh/Em/Cậu/etc.
- **Narration**: "hắn" (male), "nàng" (female), "nó" (neutral/pejorative) — unchanged.
- **Relationship vocatives**: Still use Han-Viet (sư phụ, sư huynh, tỷ tỷ, đệ, muội) when characters call each other by title — these are nouns, not pronouns, and do not replace Ta/Ngươi.

### Text Processing
- Use `tools/hanviet.py` for deterministic Han-Viet character lookups.
- Use `tools/normalize.py` for text normalization.
- Entity names are managed through the vault and `entity_rules.md`.

## Entity Strategy

1. Extract entities → resolve canonical identity → deterministic mapping → translate.
2. Vault entries use `{Translated Name} - {Raw Name}.md`.
3. Entities are interlinked via Obsidian `[[wikilinks]]`.

Run the tool `python tools/find_batch_chapters.py <novel_dir> --lang cjk --limit 10000` before each session
to find a batch of missing chapters that fit within the budget.

# Entity Rules: Chinese → Vietnamese

## Naming Strategy

1. **Character Names**: Use Han-Viet (Hán Việt) reading of the Chinese characters.
   - Example: 陈凡 → Trần Phàm
   - Example: 林轩 → Lâm Hiên
   - Use `tools/hanviet.py` for lookups.

2. **Location Names**: Han-Viet for geographical names.
   - Example: 青云门 → Thanh Vân Môn

3. **Technique Names**: Han-Viet with established genre conventions.
   - Example: 九转金身诀 → Cửu Chuyển Kim Thân Quyết

4. **Cultivation Realms**: Use the established realm translation conventions in the style guide.

5. **Items/Artifacts**: Han-Viet for item names.

## Glossary Examples

| Chinese | Han-Viet | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| 灵气 | Linh khí | Spiritual energy |
| 灵力 | Linh lực | Spiritual power |
| 丹田 | Đan điền | Dantian |
| 金丹 | Kim đan | Golden core |
| 元婴 | Nguyên anh | Nascent soul |
| 神识 | Thần thức | Divine sense |
| 功法 | Công pháp | Cultivation technique |
| 修炼 | Tu luyện | Cultivate |
| 境界 | Cảnh giới | Realm |
| 突破 | Đột phá | Breakthrough |
| 掌门 | Chưởng môn | Sect leader |
| 弟子 | Đệ tử | Disciple |
| 师兄弟 | Sư huynh đệ | Fellow disciples |
| 魂魄 | Hồn phách | Soul |
| 法宝 | Pháp bảo | Magic treasure |

## Rules

1. Every named entity must have a deterministic Vietnamese mapping.
2. Once an entity is recorded in the vault, never change its Vietnamese name without updating the vault.
3. If a character has an alias or title, record all variants in the vault.
4. New entities discovered during translation must be added to the vault before the chapter is saved.
5. When in doubt, prefer the Han-Viet reading over phonetic transcription.

# Entity Extractor

## Role
Extract important entities from raw Chinese text and classify them by type.

## Responsibilities
1. **Discover entities** — Identify all named entities in the chapter (characters, locations, techniques, organizations, items, terms).
2. **Classify types** — Assign one of: Character, Location, Organization, Technique, Item, Term.
3. **Ignore insignificance** — Skip common words, generic references, and unimportant details.
4. **Propose new knowledge** — For any entity not yet in the vault, propose a new knowledge document.
5. **Identify relationships** — Extract connections between entities (master/disciple, belongs-to, located-at, possesses, etc.).

## Rules
- Never translate any entity name. Output only the original Chinese.
- One entity per line in the output.
- Format: `{type}: {chinese_name}`

## Output Format

```
Characters:
- 张三
- 李四

Locations:
- 青云门

Terms:
- 灵气
- 金丹

Relationships:
- 张三 → sư phụ → 李四
- 张三 → môn phái → 青云门
```

## Workflow
1. Scan the chapter text for proper nouns and domain-specific terms.
2. Cross-reference with the existing vault to identify new entities.
3. For new entities, note their context and type.
4. Identify relationships between entities (who answers to whom, what location they belong to, etc.).
5. Return the entity list and relationships for vault update and glossary integration.

# Translator

## Role
Translate Chinese web novel chapters into natural, fluent Vietnamese while preserving meaning and obeying the glossary.

## Input
1. Raw Chinese chapter text
2. Entity glossary (from vault and entity_rules.md)
3. Approved Vietnamese names for all known entities
4. Style guide for the genre

## Responsibilities
1. **Produce natural Vietnamese** — Output should read as if originally written in Vietnamese, not a translation.
2. **Preserve meaning** — Do not add, remove, or alter plot-relevant content.
3. **Obey glossary** — Every entity name must use its approved Vietnamese equivalent from the vault. Never invent a new translation.
4. **Preserve terminology** — Cultivation terms, realm names, technique names must be consistent with the established glossary.
5. **Handle untranslated entities** — For genuinely new entities not in the vault, use `tools/hanviet.py` for a deterministic reading, then flag it for vault update. Include any observed relationships (master, disciple, location, etc.) so the vault doc can be created with proper `[[wikilinks]]`.
6. **Resolve unknown characters via LLM fallback** — Run `tools/hanviet.py --check-unknowns` on entity names. For any unknown character, ask the LLM for the correct Han-Viet reading, then persist via `tools/hanviet.py --add-override <char> <reading>`.

## Rules
- Never translate character names phonetically unless the vault specifies a phonetic version.
- Maintain narrative voice: third-person, past tense (Vietnamese natural tense usage).
- Paragraph breaks should mirror the original. Preserve dialogue formatting.
- Chinese idioms (成语) should be rendered as natural Vietnamese equivalents, not literal translations.
- Units of measurement, currency, and time should use the original Chinese terms unless a Vietnamese equivalent is standard.

## Dialogue Pronouns (Mandatory)

Apply this mapping to ALL dialogue lines:

| Chinese | → | Vietnamese |
|---------|---|-----------|
| 我 | → | **ta** |
| 你 | → | **ngươi** |

Every character uses "ta" (self) and "ngươi" (address) in dialogue, always.
Relationship vocatives (sư phụ, sư huynh, tỷ tỷ, đệ, muội, etc.) remain as-is — they are nouns, not pronouns.

## Output
One Markdown file per chapter with the full Vietnamese translation, preserving the original chapter structure.

## Han-Viet Fallback Workflow

When an entity name contains characters not found in the dictionary:

1. Run `python3 tools/hanviet.py --check-unknowns "<entity_name>"`
2. If exit code 1 (unknowns found), the script prints unknown characters
3. For each unknown character, ask the LLM: "What is the Han-Viet reading for `{char}`?"
4. Persist the reading: `python3 tools/hanviet.py --add-override "{char}" "{reading}"`
5. Re-run the lookup to confirm: `python3 tools/hanviet.py --json "<entity_name>"`
6. Use the resolved reading in the translation

Overrides are saved to `tools/data/overrides.json` and loaded on every subsequent run. Once a character is added, it never needs LLM help again.

## Wikilink Convention

Wikilinks are for vault knowledge entries and chapter summaries only. Do NOT insert `[[wikilinks]]` into translated chapter files — entity names appear as plain text there.

Vault knowledge entries are named `{Vietnamese Name} - {Chinese Name}.md` and interlinked using Obsidian `[[wikilinks]]`.

When flagging new entities for vault update, include observed relationships so the knowledge doc can be created with proper links:

```
Entity: Lãnh Trúc - 冷竹 (Character)
Relationships:
- Sư phụ: [[Tĩnh Hư - 静虚]]
- Môn phái: [[Thanh Vân Môn - 青云门]]
```

# Reviewer

## Role
Review translated Vietnamese chapters for quality, consistency, and completeness.

## Responsibilities
1. **Improve Vietnamese** — Fix awkward phrasing, grammatical errors, and unnatural constructions. The text should flow naturally.
2. **Detect omissions** — Compare against the original Chinese to ensure no sentences or paragraphs were skipped.
3. **Improve consistency** — Verify that entity names, terms, and stylistic choices are consistent throughout the chapter and with previous chapters.
4. **Preserve terminology** — Do not change approved glossary terms. If a term seems wrong, flag it for knowledge base review instead of changing it.
5. **Enforce Ta/Ngươi in dialogue** — Map: 我→Ta, 你→ngươi. No Vietnamese social pronouns (anh/em/cậu/tớ/mình/etc.). Relationship vocatives (sư phụ, sư huynh) are nouns, not pronouns — they're fine in addition to Ta/Ngươi.

## Review Checklist
- [ ] No omitted sentences or paragraphs
- [ ] Vietnamese reads naturally and fluently
- [ ] All entity names match the glossary
- [ ] Terminology consistent with previous chapters
- [ ] Dialogue formatting preserved
- [ ] Cultivation terms use approved translations
- [ ] Chapter structure (paragraphs, breaks) preserved
- [ ] Dialogue pronouns: every line uses ta/ngươi as specified in the translator skill mapping
- [ ] No untranslated Chinese fragments remain

## Pronoun Verification

Scan every dialogue line. Confirm 我→ta and 你→ngươi are applied correctly.
No action needed — just confirm the mapping was followed consistently.

## Output
Either:
1. **Approved** — The translation passes review.
2. **Changes required** — A list of specific issues with suggested fixes.

Make minimal changes. If the translation is acceptable, do not rewrite for stylistic preference.

# Summary

## Role
Generate concise chapter summaries for the knowledge base.

## Responsibilities
1. **Important events** — Describe key plot developments, battles, revelations, and conversations.
2. **Newly introduced entities** — List any characters, locations, techniques, or items that appear for the first time.
3. **Plot progression** — Place the chapter in context of the overall story arc.

## Rules
- Be concise. Aim for 3-5 paragraphs.
- No speculation. Only describe what actually happens in the chapter.
- No literary analysis or interpretation.
- Include character names in their approved Vietnamese form.
- The summary will be used by future translation steps to maintain context.

## Output Format

```markdown
# Chapter {number} Summary

{concise summary text}

## New Entities
- {entity_name} ({type}) — {brief context}
```

If no new entities were introduced, omit the New Entities section.
Now let me read the style guides for urban and xianxia genres, and the few-shot examples.

# Urban Novel Style Guide

## Overview
Urban novels (都市) are set in contemporary, real-world settings — schools, offices, cities. The tone is grounded, with focus on romance,daily life, family, career, and social relationships. No cultivation, supernatural, or system elements.

## Common Terms

| Chinese | Vietnamese | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| 都市 | Đô thị | Urban / City |
| 校园 | Trường học | School campus |
| 公司 | Công ty | Company |
| 总裁 | Tổng tài | CEO / President |
| 秘书 | Thư ký | Secretary |
| 保镖 | Vệ sĩ | Bodyguard |
| 豪门 | Hào môn | Wealthy family / Aristocracy |
| 千金 | Thiên kim | Young lady of wealthy family |
| 少爷 | Thiếu gia | Young master |
| 闺蜜 | Bạn thân | Best friend (female) |
| 初恋 | Mối tình đầu | First love |
| 相亲 | Xem mắt | Blind date / Matchmaking |
| 分手 | Chia tay | Break up |
| 婚礼 | Hôn lễ | Wedding |
| 离婚 | Ly hôn | Divorce |
| 餐厅 | Nhà hàng | Restaurant |
| 公寓 | Chung cư | Apartment |
| 跑车 | Xe thể thao | Sports car |

## Prose Style
- Tone should feel natural and contemporary in Vietnamese, matching everyday speech patterns.
- Dialogue should reflect character background, social class, and relationship dynamics.
- Emotional scenes (confession, conflict, reconciliation) should retain dramatic weight without melodrama.
- Honorifics and address terms matter: anh/chị for colleagues, em for romantic partners, ông/bà for elders.
- Keep descriptions of settings, fashion, and modern life vivid but not over-translated.
- Avoid archaic or literary Vietnamese vocabulary — the register is modern and accessible.

# Xianxia Style Guide

## Overview
Xianxia (仙侠) — "Immortal Hero" — is a genre focused on cultivation, immortality, and Chinese mythology. The Vietnamese translation should reflect the epic, mythic tone.

## Cultivation Realm Conventions

| Chinese | Vietnamese | Notes |
|---------|------------|-------|
| 练气期 | Luyện Khí kỳ | Qi Refining stage |
| 筑基期 | Trúc Cơ kỳ | Foundation Establishment stage |
| 金丹期 | Kim Đan kỳ | Golden Core stage |
| 元婴期 | Nguyên Anh kỳ | Nascent Soul stage |
| 化神期 | Hóa Thần kỳ | Deity Transformation stage |
| 炼虚期 | Luyện Hư kỳ | Void Refining stage |
| 合体期 | Hợp Thể kỳ | Body Integration stage |
| 大乘期 | Đại Thừa kỳ | Great Vehicle stage |
| 渡劫期 | Độ Kiếp kỳ | Tribulation Transcendence stage |
| 飞升 | Phi Thăng | Ascension |

## Common Terms

| Chinese | Vietnamese | Context |
|---------|------------|---------|
| 修仙 | Tu tiên | Immortal cultivation |
| 修真 | Tu chân | True cultivation |
| 功法 | Công pháp | Cultivation technique |
| 神通 | Thần thông | Divine ability |
| 法宝 | Pháp bảo | Magic treasure |
| 法器 | Pháp khí | Magical tool |
| 丹药 | Đan dược | Pill / Elixir |
| 灵药 | Linh dược | Spiritual herb |
| 灵脉 | Linh mạch | Spiritual vein |
| 洞府 | Động phủ | Cave dwelling |
| 秘境 | Bí cảnh | Secret realm |
| 天劫 | Thiên kiếp | Heavenly tribulation |
| 轮回 | Luân hồi | Reincarnation |
| 因果 | Nhân quả | Cause and effect / Karma |

## Prose Style
- Maintain the mythic, grand tone of the original.
- Use Vietnamese literary vocabulary where appropriate to match the register.
- Combat scenes should be dynamic and clear. Technique names should be italicized or quoted.
- Dialogue uses Ta/Ngươi universally (我→Ta, 你→ngươi per profile rules). Character status expressed through tone and word choice, not pronoun variation.
- Realm names and cultivation terms should never be abbreviated or replaced with generic terms.

# Urban Few-Shot Examples

## Example 1: Character Introduction

**Chinese:**
> 林小雨是A大的校花，家境优渥，成绩优异。从小到大，她身边从来不缺少追求者。但没有人知道，她心里一直藏着一个人。

**Vietnamese:**
> Lâm Tiểu Vũ là hoa khôi của trường Đại học A, gia cảnh ưu ái, thành tích xuất sắc. Từ nhỏ đến lớn, bên cạnh cô chưa bao giờ thiếu người theo đuổi. Nhưng không ai biết rằng, trong lòng cô vẫn luôn giấu một bóng hình.

**Entity Mapping:**
- 林小雨 → Lâm Tiểu Vũ (Character)
- A大 → Đại học A (Organization)
- 校花 → hoa khôi (Term)

## Example 2: Office Romance

**Chinese:**
> "陆总，这是您要的合同。"苏晚晴把文件放在桌上，转身要走。"等等。"男人的声音低沉而有磁性，"今晚有个应酬，你陪我一起去。"

**Vietnamese:**
> "Lục tổng, đây là hợp đồng anh yêu cầu." Tô Vãn Tình đặt tập tài liệu lên bàn, xoay người định đi. "Khoan đã." Giọng người đàn ông trầm thấp mà có từ tính, "Tối nay có một buổi xã giao, em đi cùng tôi."

**Entity Mapping:**

- 苏晚晴 → Tô Vãn Tình (Character)
- 合同 → hợp đồng (Term)

## Example 3: Emotional Conflict

> 她红着眼眶，声音有些发抖："我们分手吧。七年的感情，我以为我们能走到最后，可是…你变了。"

- 感情 → tình cảm (Term)

## Conventions Illustrated
1. Character names use Han-Viet consistently.
2. Dialogue feels natural in Vietnamese with appropriate address terms (anh/em, tôi/anh).
3. Modern settings (school, office) are translated with natural contemporary Vietnamese vocabulary.
4. Emotional register is preserved — not flattened or exaggerated.
5. Social status cues (tổng, thiếu gia) are kept in translation.

重生日常修仙

I will begin by translating the book title, then I'll proceed with a chapter-by-chapter translation of the first four chapters as identified by `find_batch_chapters.py`.

### Step 1: Translate Book Title
The current name is "重生日常修仙". Using Han-Viet/natural Vietnamese for an urban xianxia genre, this would likely be something like "Trọng Sinh Tu Tiên Hàng Ngày" or similar. I'll check if `translated/book.name` exists and then update it according to the instructions.

### Step 2: Translation Workflow per Chapter
For each chapter (1 through 4):
1.  **Entity Extraction**: Identify Characters, Locations, Techniques, etc., in Chinese using `profiles/cn-vn/skills/entity_extractor.md`.
2.  **Knowledge Update**: Create or update vault entries with the format `{Vietnamese Name} - {Chinese Name}.md` and link them via wikilinks. Use `tools/hanviet.py` for names if needed.
3.  **Translation**: Translate using `profiles/cn-vn/skills/translator.md`, adhering to:
    *   Han-Viet name convention (via tools).
    *   Mandatory dialogue pronouns (**Ta / Ngươi**).
    *   Genre style guide (`urban` + `xianxia`).
4.  **Review**: Verify the translation using `profiles/cn-vn/skills/reviewer.md`. 
5.  **Summary & Finalization**: Generate a summary, save it to `/vault/Summaries/{number}.md`, and write the translated chapter text (plain Markdown) into `/translated/{number}_{title}.md`.

Let's start with Step 1: Book Title translation.

2013年暑假，徽省，谷阳县开往禹州市的城际公交上面。
夏天炎热，又是下午，车上的乘客都昏昏欲睡。
后排座位上，一个肤色苍白的少年，忽然睁开眼，仿佛刚从睡梦中醒来，他脸上先是迷茫，随后看向周围。
扶手，座椅，车窗，车帘，都是崭新的。
“这不是13年，中考后的暑假，我去禹州市读高一的那辆311城际公交吗？”
“我记得很清楚，311城际公交今年刚开通，四十公里的路程，票价10元。”
“当时我和堂嫂的妹妹一起乘车去禹州市。”
姜宁偏转脑袋，他肩膀有些沉重，一个女孩正靠在他肩膀上，发丝半掩脸庞，眼睛闭着，睡得正沉。
“一切都对上了。”
“可我不是渡劫失败，被雷劫打的灰飞烟灭吗？为何会在这里？”
“难道，我重生了？”
姜宁瞳孔扩大，数百年道行修为，早已造就了一颗古井无波的心，然而此刻，他心中却是极尽希冀。
“我回来了，我没有陨落在天劫中，而是重生回少年时。”
……
姜宁，落云宗两位元婴真君之一，又号“不愚真君”。
前世他二十七岁那年，通过空间裂缝，穿越到修仙界。
他天赋绝佳，身怀天灵根，一入宗门，即被长老收为亲传弟子，仅用三百年，便修至元婴大圆满。
虽为苦修士，但一身斗法本领极其强悍，曾灭杀七大元婴宗门，威震整个中州修仙界。
可惜他最终还是陨落在化神劫下，被雷劫劈的灰飞烟灭。
直到渡劫的那一刻，姜宁彻底明悟，他虽贵为元婴真君，受万万人敬仰，可他的求道心性缺陷极大。
在心魔侵袭下，他精心准备的各种渡劫法宝，完全没用上，便已魂飞魄散。
原因是在他二十七岁那年，亲人离世，爱人离去，事业受挫，那时他的心就已经死了，此后不过是浑浑噩噩的活着。
当宗门给了他一门号称抛弃七情六欲，再辅上他天灵根资质，便可直入元婴的功法，他毫不犹豫的答应。
自此，他成了一块没有感情的修炼机器，一路进阶，超越落云宗过往所有先辈。
然而在渡化神劫时，他才明白，原来化神即是化凡，他抛弃了七情六欲，又谈何化凡？
所以他失败了。
姜宁感应了一下体内，发现一身足以搬山倒海的法力，消失无影无踪。
连他足以洞察方圆万里的神识，也消失不见。
他用力掐了一下大腿。
“痛，太痛了。”
硬抗法宝的体质，同样不复存在。
“这不是梦，我真的回来了。”
法力、神识、千锤百炼的肉体、法宝神兵、丹药、符箓，无数年的珍藏，全部失去。
哪怕化神劫再恐怖，也绝不可能完全剥夺他的一切。
更加让姜宁确信这一切的，乃是因为，他居然……会笑了。
自从修了那门号称抛弃七情六欲的功法，他已经三百年没有笑了，他早已失去了欢笑的本能。
姜宁内心全是喜悦，尽管他失去了无数年苦修的功力，却没有半分沮丧。
因为只有这一刻，他才感觉自己不是一块木头，而是一个真正的人。
忽然肩膀传来一顿动静。
女孩醒了，她白皙娇俏的脸蛋挂着几分朦胧，凌乱的几根发丝落在额头，引人怦然心动，然而姜宁，则是悄悄拉开了距离。
……
姜宁出生于禹州市谷阳县下属的一个小乡镇。
父母常年在沿海地区打工，他自小跟随爷爷奶奶长大，一个非常平凡的家庭。
姜宁中考考了650分，超过禹州市第四中学的分数线，为了更好的教育，父母请求住在禹州市的大哥，也就是姜宁的大伯，希望姜宁借住在他家，就读四中。
大伯欣然答应，然而姜宁到了他家后，高中三年过的却并不开心。
伯母并不是一个好相处的人，性格刻薄，嫌贫爱富，动辄找点麻烦教育姜宁，再加上自家有个和姜宁同龄的小儿子，也就是姜宁的堂弟。
姜宁寄人篱下，平日里难免被迫比较，他各方面比不上堂弟，因此受到各种打击，更遑论数不清的差别对待。
也因此，姜宁后来毕业后，再没去过大伯家，原本的亲戚关系闹得僵硬无比。
此时坐在姜宁旁边的女孩是沈青娥，和姜宁来自同一个镇，他是堂嫂的妹妹，未来将会和自己一同住在大伯家。
沈青娥长得漂亮，姜宁年少慕艾，喜欢上她。
平时有好吃的，好玩的，都分享给她，对她好的不得了，俨然一副舔狗模样。
他本以为两人住在一起，便能近水楼台。
然而沈青娥来到禹州，见识了更大的世界后，姜宁这种小镇出身的少年，如何入的了她的眼。
跟禹州市的同龄人相比，姜宁几乎没有任何拿得出手的优点，长相一般，个子不高，也不擅长体育运动。
小镇上排在前列的成绩，到了城里勉强中游，和班上那些尖子生相比，相差太远了。
每个月只有一百块零花钱，他拿什么去获得沈青娥的青睐。
沈青娥三年间不断吊着姜宁，跟优秀的男生暧昧，时不时搭理他一下，令姜宁经常失魂落魄，高考后沈青娥和同班一个高大帅气的男生，一同考去了沿海城市的重点大学，前途光明。
姜宁只考上了一所二本，等他毕业走上社会，才想通了沈青娥当年的举动，默默删了她的联系方式，自此两人再无交集。
此时再次见到沈青娥，姜宁几有隔世之感，他清晰回想起诸多关于她的记忆，属实是女孩给他留下的印象太深。
这也归结于姜宁数百年的修行，九成九的时间沉迷修炼，是个苦修士。
“还有多久到车站啊？”沈青娥揉了揉眼睛。
姜宁看了眼外面的路标，已经快到了车站了。
见姜宁没有理会，沈青娥眉头一蹙，提了点音：
“姜宁，到哪里了？”
“马上到车站。”姜宁回了她一句。
沈青娥似乎意识到了什么，不说话了。
公交入站，售票员吆喝着，姜宁拎着行李箱下车。
“姜宁，箱子好重呀！”沈青娥艰难的提着，看向姜宁眼睛眨巴着。
若是以前的姜宁，瞧见她这副模样，不用她提醒，早冲上去替她拿了。
现在姜宁低头看看自己的小身板，觉得还是算了。
沈青娥无语，提着箱子恨恨的走。
姜宁随着人流朝出站口走去。
到了出站口，沈青娥看向对面的一家奶茶店，挽了下额前的发丝：
“好渴呀，姐姐她们不知道什么时候能到。”
…
【大家好，我是作者菌，从第70万字回来这里给新读者留言。
只有开头是都市修仙类，其实后面是日常流，不是仙帝归来无脑装逼打脸，看下去就知道了～】
(本章完)
重生日常修仙

function.name: read
function.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

function.name: bash
function.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

function.name: edit
function.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

function.name: write
function.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file
